steps.title: Extract the geometric constraint from parallel lines
steps.content:
When transversal $s$ cuts parallel lines $q$ and $r$, it creates angles at two intersection points.

From the figure, angles $x$ and $y$ are positioned such that they satisfy a geometric relationship.

By analyzing the configuration of these angles relative to the parallel lines, we determine that:
$$x + y = 155°$$

steps.title: Combine the geometric and algebraic equations
steps.content:
We now have a system of two equations:
  • Algebraic: $y = 2x + 8$ (given)
  • Geometric: $x + y = 155$ (from the figure)
These two constraints together determine a unique value for $x$. The fastest approach is to substitute one equation into the other.

steps.title: Solve using substitution
steps.content:
Substitute $y = 2x + 8$ into $x + y = 155$:
$$x + (2x + 8) = 155$$
$$3x + 8 = 155$$
$$3x = 147$$
$$x = 49$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
